MINI MINUTES:
APPEARANCES VIA ZOOM VIDEOCONFERENCE:
Sahar Malek, Esq. (310.844.9696; sahar@aswtlawyers.com) of ARIAS SANGUINETTI WANG & TORRIJOS LLP for Plaintiff.
Jorge Guardado, Esq. (213.528.8858; Jorge@thetranfirm.com) of THE TRAN FIRM for Plaintiff.

A R G U E D. (as fully reported in the reporter's notes/transcript) The Court having reviewed the pleadings and having heard oral argument rules as follows:

DEFENDANT SAFEWAY INC. and ELENA ARROYO-ASATO'S Motion For Judgment On The Pleadings is taken under submission.

Counsel for Plaintiff to submit a proposed order in Word format, tracking the court's tentative ruling to contestdept302tr@sftc.org.

Judge: Richard B. Ulmer Jr., Clerk: A. Datt, Court Reporter: Maria Torreano, CSR#8600, maria.torreano@gmail.com, Reported. =(302/RBU)

Motion For Judgment On The Pleadings (Motions)
Off Calendar Apr-07-2022 Off Calendar
MINI MINUTES:

APPEARANCES (VIA ZOOM VIDEOCONFERENCE):
Atty. CHRISTOPHER DENGLER, Esq. of Littler Mendelson, P.C. (Tel: 213-443-4300) for defendants Safeway Inc. and Elena Arroyo-Asato;
No other appearances made in this matter.

HEARING RE: DEFENDANTS SAFEWAY INC. AND ELENA ARROYO-ASATO's Motion For Judgment On The Pleadings.

Matter not contested/argued. The Court adopts its tentative ruling.

Matter is off calendar. CCP 439(a) requires a meet and confer in person or by telephone. It appears that the parties only met and conferred by letter and e-mail. The parties are ordered to meet and confer to see whether they can agree to the filing of an amended complaint if the date regarding the right to sue letter is in fact pleaded erroneously.
